State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901         322-6441           322-6444


June 25, 1981


Mr. Ross B. Kongable, Chairman
CITIZENS FOR THE PRESERVATION
OF EL SEGUNDO
909 McCarthy Street
El Segundo, CA 90245

Dear Mr. Kongable:

     Thank you for your request for an advisory opinion.
Unfortunately, the Commission is only authorized to render
opinions or to give written advice to persons having duties under
the Political Reform Act or their authorized representatives.
Government Code Section 83114.

     Consequently, since you have no duty under the Act and are
not the authorized representative of those who may have duties,
then we are not authorized to render such advice. However, if the
School Board or its representative should request our advice, then
we could render it to them. Perhaps you can convince them that
such advice should be sought. I have brought this matter to the
attention of the Los Angeles County Counsel's Office.

     Otherwise, if you feel that a violation of the Act has occurred,
then you can file a complaint with our Enforcement Division, at
this same address. They would have no jurisdiction over the issue
of a gift of public funds.

     I trust that this provides some guidance for you on this
matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Robert E. Leidigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

REL;gs

May 28, 1981
909 Moorthy Ct.
El Segundo, Ca. :90245

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, CA. 95814

Dear Sirs:

I would like to get an advisory opinion concerning in-kind
campaign contributions.

About a year ago, the El Segundo Unified School District entered
into a lease option contract with a private developer to build
condominiums on school property. The contract provided for
option payments until such time as the land was rezoned by
the city for that purpose.

Community objections were disregarded during the course of the
public hearings, and approval of the re-zoning ordinance resulted in the circulation of referendum petitions. Although
a committee was formed consisting of city and school officials
to discourage citizens from signing the referendum petition,
it should qualify with approximately three times the required
number of signatures.

At the School Board meeting of May 27, 1981, the developer requested that his options be extended for six months without
payment of the $10,000 per month as called for in the contract.
He stated that he "intended to spend a considerable sum for
education of the voters of El Segundo". When the Board replied
that they were not sure they could give away "a options for
no money", the developer suggested that he pay $1.00 for the
agreement. The School Superintendent replied that the actual
value of the property was worth $21,000 per month and that
it (the request) would be a gift of public funds.

The matter was resolved when the Board President said, "We
cannot spend the money to educate them(the voters), but you
(the developer) can." Several references were made both by the
Board and the developer to the fact that the task of educating
the voters was to be a "partnership". The Board voted to
approve an extension of the option for two weeks, without a
fee, "until our lawyers can work it out".

By allowing the developer to use the option funds for two weeks
for campaign purposes instead of enforcing the contract, it
contributed $5,000 to his campaign costs. By the superintendent's
statement, the contribution made by the board would be worth
$11,500 for the two weeks (June 1 to Jun 15, 1981). On June 9,
1981, the Board will consider extending the option for the
duration of the referendum campaign at no fee. That would be a
value of about $50,000 to $60,000 to the developer, and cost
the district in excess of $100,000 in lost revenue. An expenditure of approximately $20 per voter should enable the developer
to reverse the existing community opinion which is about 80%
in opposition.

QUESTIONS:
Would the granting of the option for two weeks constitute an
in-kind contribution by the Board of Education? (The intent
of the monies to be used to educate the voters was repeatedly
stated throughout the public Board meeting).
IF SO, who is responsible for filing the financial disclosure
forms for the initial pledge of $5,000 (or $11,500)?
Would the Board be considered a major donor? or the Developer?
Would the in-kind contribution be valued at the option fee of
$10,000 per month, or the $21,000 per month that it cost the
taxpayers?

Thanking you for your consideration and timely response,

-Ross B. Wongable, Chmn
CITIZENS FOR THE PRESERVATION OF EL SEGUNDO

                              Attorney for the Preservation

Type of Question:

Advice: Ad said its nearly winter 21,000 months

board & desks - no fee to school board for dwel.

sections.question:
Would the granting of the option for no fee constitute an in-kind contribution by the Board of Education? (The intent of the monies to be used to educate the voters was repeatedly stated throughout the public Board meeting).
IF SO, who is responsible for filing the financial disclosure forms for the initial pledge of $5,000 (or $11,500)?
Would the Board be considered a major donor? or the Developer?
Would the in-kind contribution be valued at the option fee of $10,000 per month, or the $21,000 per month that it cost the taxpayers?

Thanking you for your consideration and timely response,

ROSS B. RONGABLE, Chmn  
CITIZENS FOR THE PRESERVATION OF EL SEGUNDO

This document is a transmittal of correspondence dated 5/28, with the subject line "Advice - No-kind campaign contributions." The correspondent is listed as Leon B. Kangable, El Segundo. The document lists several names under different categories: Administration (Bob T., Linda, Bev), Executive (Tom H., Ted, Nancy W., Bob S., John M.), Conflicts/Legal (Barbara, Diane, Stella, Alice, Jeanette, Bob L.), Enforcement (Bob B.), TA&A (Lynn, Carla, Maryann), and others (JAY, RICH, HELEN-L., JEANNE).

The information section states that a request for advice must be answered within 21 days. It instructs to take whatever action you consider appropriate, reply direct by 6/35, copy to [illegible], prepare suggested reply and return to [illegible], and coordinate reply with [illegible].

Handwritten notes below the typed section read:

"No advice letter unless school bd or Co. Counsel requests.

Tell Co. Counsel if looks like a problem may want to seek advice. Tony Alpern (213) 485-4230 City #179

If further contact them worry about it then.

Letter to Kangable sitting 18420 & also say no advice to him but maybe to them—if they keep up, he can complain.

6/25 Edward Pozarski—LA Co. Counsel's Ofc. 974-1835

Advised him re issues. He says no time for preventative advice. Layoffs on the way for Co. Counsel's in that area of the office (Schools Division) (a lot) for every 10-12 dists."

The handwritten notes appear to be additional instructions or observations related to the advice request and the situation involving Leon B. Kangable.

TELEPHONE ADVICE

Attorney: B. Milman
Date: 5-28-81
Advice Letter: Sent Request
Person Requesting Advice: Ross Korgabel
Citizens for the Preservation of El Segundo
Telephone:

Type of Question:
El Segundo - referendum process against zoning ordinance. School Bd has K Co. developer to build on property - have an option - council had changed zoning to allow this. At schoolbd developer requested 3rd extension - pays $10,000/month - ref. will take 3 mos. He is now asked for 6 mo. extension w/o pay.
Advice: Bd said it's really worth $1,000/month.
Educating voters would be "partnership" - school board & devel. - no fee to school board from devel. Instead he would use the $ for campaigning - use it now for 2 weeks - a "contribution" to camp. conts.

We only give advice to people who have duties. We can act on enforcement comp. We can complain to DA. He can ask School Bd or its counsel us for advice.

enforcement - school board can give cash for advice
